Conditions we treat

What's keeping you off your feet?

From a stubborn ingrown nail to surgical bunion correction, all diagnosed and treated in one Midtown office with digital X-ray on site.

Bunions & hammertoes

Painful deformities corrected through keyhole incisions, so most patients recover faster with less pain.

↗

Heel pain & plantar fasciitis

Shockwave therapy, injections, and 3D-printed custom orthotics for pain that won't quit.

↗

Ingrown & fungal nails

Fast, lasting relief and restored, healthy-looking nails, no matter how far gone they seem.

↗

Plantar warts

SWIFT microwave therapy clears stubborn warts at the source. No cutting, no downtime.

↗

Sports injuries

Sprains, strains, and fractures, diagnosed on the spot with in-office digital X-ray, then taped, treated, and back in training.

↗

Custom orthotics

3D-scanned and 3D-printed to your exact foot, with support engineered for the way you actually walk.

↗

The signature difference

Minimally invasive surgery, done right.

Traditional bunion surgery means a long incision, a visible scar, and weeks of painful recovery. Dr. Farooqi corrects bunions, hammertoes, and heel spurs through incisions just millimeters long, guided by live imaging.

Trained in both open and minimally invasive techniques during a three-year surgical residency at Bellevue Hospital, he'll recommend what's genuinely right for your foot, not what's easiest to bill.

Ask about MIS at your consult
Traditional open surgeryone long incision
Minimally invasive surgerya few millimeters

Illustrative comparison. Incision size and approach vary by procedure and patient. Discussed candidly at your consultation.

Smaller incisionsLess visible scarring
Less painMany patients need fewer painkillers
Faster recoveryBack to life, sooner

Meet your doctor

Care that starts with actually listening.

Dr. Farooqi has practiced podiatric medicine in New York City for over a decade. Patients consistently mention the same two things: he takes the time to explain what's going on, and his hands match his bedside manner. His office sits at Lexington & 40th, steps from Grand Central.

Insurance & payment

Good to know

Questions, answered.

What happens at my first visit?+

A thorough exam and an honest conversation. If imaging is needed, digital X-rays are taken on the spot, so you leave with a diagnosis and a clear plan, not a referral slip and another appointment somewhere else.

Do I need a referral?+

Most plans let you see a podiatrist directly, though some HMO plans require a referral. Call the office with your insurance details and we'll help you check before you book.

How is minimally invasive surgery different?+

Instead of one long incision, corrections are made through openings just millimeters wide. For most patients that means less visible scarring, less post-operative pain, and a quicker return to normal life. Recovery time varies by procedure, and Dr. Farooqi will walk you through exactly what to expect at your consultation.

Can I be seen quickly?+

Same-week appointments are usually available, and the schedule is built around Midtown work hours: early mornings on Monday, evenings on Thursday, and virtual visits on Friday. For urgent issues, call and we'll do our best to fit you in.

What if my deductible is high or I'm uninsured?+

Reasonable self-pay rates are available. Just ask when booking. We also work with Workers' Compensation and No-fault claims.
